name: SOL Deep Value Reclaim v1
thesis: SOL 4h is the best-fit primary lane for testing trend pullback continuation after a deeper value reclaim into structural support. The edge should come from broad reclaimable pullbacks, not from shallow EMA-touch continuation or single-bar hero entries.
invalidation_condition: Abort if deeper pullback reclaims remain too sparse, if reclaimed support still mean-reverts without continuation, or if follow-through is too small after costs.

pre_submit_reasoning.market_behavior_targeted: Price overshoots below short-term value, reclaims structural support, and then continues modestly as short-term balance is restored.
pre_submit_reasoning.why_edge_should_exist: In a mixed chop/transition tape, deeper pullbacks can clear weak positioning and create cleaner continuation entries than shallow momentum chasing.
pre_submit_reasoning.asset_timeframe_fit: SOL 4h is a good home because it tends to overshoot enough for reclaim structure to be visible without relying on rare crash-only conditions.
pre_submit_reasoning.likely_failure_mode: If the tape remains purely rotational, reclaimed support may only produce a bounce to value and not enough continuation.
pre_submit_reasoning.first_refinement_path: If entries engage but continuation is shallow, keep the reclaim logic and shorten the back-half exit ambition.

density_sanity_check.contradictory_filters: none obvious; pullback and reclaim are sequential and broad
density_sanity_check.event_sparsity: managed with moderate reclaim depth instead of rare capitulation requirements
